zh.siteSubtitle: 拔摩岛的约翰（约公元95年）
zh.homeIntro1: 《启示录》（希腊语 Apokalypsis，意为「揭示」）是《新约圣经》的最后一卷书，传统上认为由使徒约翰在罗马皇帝多米提安统治末期（约公元95年）流放于拔摩岛期间写成。这部书以书信形式开始，随后展开一系列宏大的末世异象。
zh.homeIntro2: 全书以象征性的数字和图像构建了一幅末世画卷：七封印揭开灾难序幕，四骑士带来征服、战争、饥荒与死亡，七号角吹响更深重的审判，兽的印记「666」标记邪恶的权势，巴比伦大城代表堕落的文明，哈米吉多顿是善恶最终决战之地。
zh.homeIntro3: 《启示录》最终以光明收尾：基督再临、千禧年国度、最后审判，以及新天新地中降下的新耶路撒冷——一座不再有死亡、悲哀和痛苦的圣城。两千年来，这部书深刻影响了西方文明的末世观、艺术创作与政治想象，是人类历史上最具影响力的启示文学。
zh.coreQuote: 「看哪，我将一切都更新了。」——启示录 21:5
keyPointsList: 致七教会书信 —— 对小亚细亚七座教会的训诫与应许（启1-3章）
七封印与四骑士 —— 揭开天上书卷，四骑士带来征服、战争、饥荒与死亡（启6章）
七号角 —— 天使吹号降下冰雹火焰、海水变血、星辰坠落等灾难（启8-11章）
龙与兽 —— 撒但（大红龙）与兽（666）的权势兴起（启12-13章）
巴比伦倾覆 —— 代表堕落文明的巴比伦大城在一时之间毁灭（启17-18章）
哈米吉多顿 —— 善恶最终决战，基督得胜（启19章）
新耶路撒冷 —— 新天新地降临，不再有死亡和眼泪（启21-22章）
sourceList: 预言原文来自《新约圣经·启示录》各主要译本（BibleGateway 和合本）
学术背景参考《启示录》研究综述（Wikipedia）
验证评价基于历史事件比照与主流释经学观点，编者观点不代表任何教派立场
站点图标采用 Alpha-Omega (AO) 符号——启示录中上帝自称「我是阿拉法，我是俄梅戛，是首先的，是末后的」，这是启示录最具标志性的符号

en.homeTitle: What is the Book of Revelation?
en.homeIntro1: The Book of Revelation (Greek: Apokalypsis, meaning 'unveiling') is the final book of the New Testament, traditionally attributed to the Apostle John, written during his exile on the island of Patmos near the end of Emperor Domitian's reign (circa 95 AD). It begins as a letter to seven churches and unfolds into a series of grand apocalyptic visions.
en.homeIntro2: The text constructs an eschatological panorama through symbolic numbers and imagery: seven seals unleash catastrophe, four horsemen bring conquest, war, famine, and death, seven trumpets sound deeper judgments, the mark of the Beast '666' signifies evil power, Babylon the Great represents fallen civilization, and Armageddon is the site of the ultimate battle between good and evil.
en.homeIntro3: Revelation concludes with light: the Second Coming of Christ, the Millennial Kingdom, the Last Judgment, and the descent of New Jerusalem in a new heaven and new earth — a holy city where death, sorrow, and pain are no more. For two millennia, this book has profoundly shaped Western civilization's eschatology, art, and political imagination, making it the most influential apocalyptic text in human history.
en.coreQuote: "Behold, I am making all things new." — Revelation 21:5
keyPointsList: Letters to Seven Churches —— Admonitions and promises to seven churches in Asia Minor (Rev 1-3)
Seven Seals & Four Horsemen —— Opening the heavenly scroll; four horsemen bring conquest, war, famine, and death (Rev 6)
Seven Trumpets —— Angels sound trumpets bringing hail, fire, seas of blood, and falling stars (Rev 8-11)
Dragon & Beast —— Satan (great red dragon) and the Beast (666) rise to power (Rev 12-13)
Fall of Babylon —— Babylon the Great, symbol of fallen civilization, destroyed in a single hour (Rev 17-18)
Armageddon —— The final battle between good and evil; Christ is victorious (Rev 19)
New Jerusalem —— A new heaven and new earth descend; no more death or tears (Rev 21-22)
sourceList: Prophecy texts from the Book of Revelation, New Testament, across major translations (BibleGateway NIV)
Academic context from scholarly overviews of Revelation (Wikipedia)
Verification based on historical events and mainstream hermeneutics; editorial views do not represent any denominational position
Site icon: Alpha-Omega (AO) symbol — in Revelation, God declares 'I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last,' making this the book's most iconic symbol
